danger_signals.visual_form: On-screen arrow marking fleeing suspect's car position
danger_signals.meaning: Target location and direction; active threat requiring pursuit
Imminent failure state; criminal escape risk escalating

danger_signals.visual_form: Suspect vehicles in distinct colors (white, yellow, silver, blue, red) vs. player's black Porsche
danger_signals.meaning: Visual differentiation of enemy/threat; high-speed evasion target
Performance degradation; increases criminal escape risk

danger_signals.visual_form: Branching paths requiring correct navigation choice
danger_signals.meaning: Wrong turn extends pursuit distance; tactical decision failure

safety_signals.visual_form: Reaching suspect vehicle triggers timer reset (60-70 additional seconds)
Player drives black Porsche 928; HQ radio contact from Nancy
safety_signals.meaning: Institutional legitimacy; law enforcement role established
Safe state transition; mission objective achieved

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Radio communications from Nancy at HQ; official pursuit authorization

faction_indicators.color_identity: Multiple: white, yellow, silver, blue, red per suspect
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: High-performance sports cars (Lotus Esprit, Lamborghini Countach, Porsche 911, Ferrari 288 GTO, Porsche 928)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Each level features distinct criminal archetype with vehicle color-coding
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Fleeing behavior; distance separation on track; evasion patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Criminal vehicle visible ahead with directional arrow; on-screen distance marker; speedometer showing high velocity (300+ km/h)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Criminal vehicle maintains constant forward motion away from player; attempts to maximize separation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Suspect car physically reachable on-screen; collision damage visible; vehicle performance degradation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Criminal vehicle slows or stops after repeated ramming; arrest animation triggers upon sufficient impact damage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Timer visibly depleting; countdown acceleration visual/audio cues
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must increase speed; wrong turns become catastrophic; criminal escape becomes probable

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Time expires; criminal vehicle exits screen boundary
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Level restart; loss of mission objective; score penalty

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Suspect arrested; transition to next level scenario; briefing from Nancy on new criminal
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Time resets; new criminal profile introduced; new vehicle color and geographic location established

environmental_storytelling.example: Nancy at Chase HQ provides criminal profile, vehicle description, and geographic location at level start; establishes institutional context and mission legitimacy
Each criminal archetype assigned distinct vehicle color (Ralph=white Lotus, Carlos=yellow Lamborghini, etc.); visual taxonomy aids quick threat recognition
Levels named after cities with associated criminal stereotypes (Idaho Slasher, New York armed robber, Chicago pushers, L.A. kidnapper, Eastern Bloc Spy); establishes implicit crime landscape
Suspect vehicles are high-performance sports cars; establishes criminals as wealthy/sophisticated; escalates from Lotus to Ferrari to Porsche in power hierarchy
60-70 second windows communicate imminent danger; criminals are active threats requiring immediate apprehension, not passive targets

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Constant forward acceleration away from player; maintains maximum distance; exploits road forks to extend pursuit
Visible slowing after repeated collisions; reduced evasion efficiency; eventual halt upon sufficient damage accumulation
Accelerates to chase; follows criminal; capable of ramming; responds to player steering input at high speed
Radio communication; provides criminal briefing; establishes pursuit objectives via ham radio frequencies (144-148 MHz authentic detail)

notes: Chase H.Q. communicates world-state primarily through temporal pressure (timer mechanics) and visual differentiation (vehicle colors, on-screen positioning). The game operates within a police procedural framework where institutional authority (Chase H.Q., Nancy, police radio) legitimizes player actions. Criminal profiles are stereotyped by geography and vehicle class, creating a loose narrative taxonomy. The black vs. colored vehicle distinction is the primary safety/danger signal. Genesis version release (1992-1993) post-dates original arcade but maintains core visual language. Limited environmental storytelling; world-state conveyed through mission briefings and chase mechanics rather than world-building details.
